overview: These appear to be Indrella ampulla, also known as the Variable Forest Snail or Red Giant Snail. They are endemic to the Western Ghats of India and are known for their distinct color morphs, including red, orange, and yellow. The image shows them engaged in mating, during which they display a characteristic "face-to-face" behavior. The purple structures visible are their phalli, which evert from the right side of the head during copulation. Instagram +3 4 sites The Variable Forest Snail, also known as the Red Giant Snail ...
